title: rhus punjabensis var. pilosa
intro: Rhus punjabensis var. pilosa, also known as the hairy sumac or punjab sumac, is a plant species belonging to the Anacardiaceae family, which is native to the Indian subcontinent and parts of the Middle East. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rhus punjabensis var. pilosa include reducing fever, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and alleviating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations like teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in the form of powders and ointments for topical application.
